pages_reviewed: index.html (code review — CSS vs DESIGN.md audit)
sitewide footer consistency audit (5 main pages + standalone pages)
agents.html (code review — warroom.js BIOS array vs DESIGN.md agent identities)

tickets_written.summary: Orphan 'Veteran Studs' chip on discovery filters — 6th chip wraps to centered second row alone
Mini-screener table rows use 1px solid borders instead of 2px dashed — DESIGN.md violation
--ink-light is #6d5a4d but DESIGN.md says #8a7565 — 219 usages wrong, kills typographic hierarchy
Watermark implemented 5 different ways across 15 pages — 3 fonts, 4 opacities, 4 z-indexes. Undermines brand on Reddit screenshots.
Prompts page has minimal 2-line footer instead of full 6-column nav footer — conversion dead end for SEO funnel traffic
3 of 6 agent emojis/animals wrong on Situation Room — Dolphin shown as Owl, Fox shown as Bear, Octopus shown as Fox. Home page contradicts agents page.

notes: Production site (razzle.lol) returned blank HTML in headless browser — may be down or blocking headless
Backend not running locally so all data-dependent pages show error states (expected)
Dark mode toggle on mobile nav has only 6px right margin — tight but not clipped
Fake testimonials on home page will be credibility risk on Reddit
Agent name inconsistency: DESIGN.md says 'The Fox', agent-config.js says 'Bones', home page says 'THE DIPLOMAT' — now partially covered by DES-006 (emojis/animals), naming decision still open
--ink-light now ticketed as DES-003 — sitewide P1
1px solid borders on data rows appear in compare.html and tradevalues.html too — same pattern as DES-002 but sitewide
tradevalues.html has redirect to lab.html when loaded standalone — consolidation pattern

Footer audit: 4 main pages have full 6-col footer (index, agents, league-intel, pricing). prompts.html has minimal footer. ~60 standalone panel pages have minimal footer too (acceptable since they redirect to lab.html). about.html has full 6-col footer now.
Dark mode --ink-light mismatch already covered by DES-003
Pricing page looks clean — feature matrix, FAQ, interval toggle, mobile breakpoints all solid
